data.title: Weekly economic calendar
data.description:
Monday - February 24

08:30 AM Chicago Fed National Activity Index

The Chicago Fed National Activity Index (CFNAI) is a monthly index that tracks overall economic activity and inflationary pressures. The CFNAI is a weighted average of 85 existing monthly indicators of national economic activity. The index recorded positive growth in January after 6 months of decline.

10:30 AM Dallas Fed Manufacturing Survey

The Dallas Fed Manufacturing Survey tracks factory activity in Texas on a monthly basis. Firms are asked whether output, employment, orders, prices and other indicators increased, decreased or remained unchanged over the previous month. The general business activity index reported growth for the past 2 months.

11:30 AM 2-Yr Note Auction

The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $69 billion of 2-year Floating Rate Notes. US 2 Year Note Bond Yield was 4.21 percent on Monday February 24, according to over-the-counter interbank yield quotes for this government bond maturity.

Tuesday - February 25

4:20 AM Fed's Logan Speech

Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan speaks before the 2025 BEAR (Bank of England Agenda for Research) Conference:"The Future of the Central Bank Balance Sheet".

9:00 AM Case-Shiller Home Price Index
The S&P Corelogic Case-Shiller home price index tracks monthly changes in the value of residential real estate in 20 metropolitan regions across the nation. Index is expected to grow 4.4% Y/Y Month on month, the index was up 0.4%.
9:00 AM FHFA House Price Index
The Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) House Price Index (HPI) covers single-family housing, using data provided by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
10:00 AM Consumer Confidence
The Conference Board's confidence report surveys consumers on their assessments of the labor market, business activity, and their own financial conditions. The consensus looks for consumer confidence to extend its fall in February 103.0.
10:00 AM Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index
This survey tracks business conditions in the Richmond Fed's manufacturing sector. The headline index is a composite of the new orders, shipments, and employment indexes.

1:00 PM 5-Year Note Auction

The auction indicates the percentage yield on five 5-year US Treasury notes. The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $70 billion of 5-year notes. According to over-the-counter interbank yield quotes for this government bond maturity, the US 5-year note bond yield was 4.29 percent on Monday, February 24.

1:00 PM Money Supply
Money supply is in terms of two components—M1 and M2. M1 and M2 are progressively more inclusive measures of money: M1 is included in M2. M1, the more narrowly defined measure, consists of the most liquid forms of money, namely currency and checkable deposits. The non-M1 components of M2 are primarily household holdings of savings deposits, small time deposits, and retail money market mutual funds.
1:00 PM Fed's Barkin: "Inflation Then and Now"
Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin speaks on "Inflation Then and Now" before the Rotary Club of Richmond.
Wednesday - February 26

07:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The data point measures the change in the number of new applications for mortgages backed by the MBA during the reported week. Mortgage applications fell 6.6% in the last week after interest rates softened to 6.93%.

8:30 AM Fed' Barkin: "Inflation Then and Now"

Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin speaks on "Inflation Then and Now" before the Northern Virginia Chamber of Commerce.

10:00 AM New Home Sales

New home sales measure the number of newly constructed homes with a committed sale during the month. Home sales are seen falling back to a 677,000 annual rate in January after a sharp rise to 698,000 in December.

10:30 AM EIA Petroleum Inventories

The Energy Information Administration weekly data reports inventory levels of US crude oil, gasoline and distillates stocks. The figure shows how much oil and products are available in storage. U.S. commercial crude oil inventories increased by 4.6 million barrels in the last week.

11:00 AM Survey of Business Uncertainty

The survey of business uncertainty is a panel survey measuring year-ahead expectations that US firms have about their own sales and employment growth. The sample covers all regions of the US economy, all industries except agriculture and government, and a broad range of firm sizes.

11:30 AM 2-Yr FRN Note Auction

The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $28 billion of 2-year Floating Rate Notes.

12:00 PM Fed's Bostic Speech

Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic participates in conversation on the economic outlook and housing before the Urban Land Institute's annual Housing Opportunity Conference.

1:00 PM 7-Year Note Auction

The auction indicates the percentage yield on seven 7-year US Treasury notes. The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $44 billion of 7-year notes. According to over-the-counter interbank yield quotes for this government bond maturity, the US 7-year note bond yield was 4.37 percent on Monday, February 24.

Thursday - February 27

8:30 AM Durable Goods

Durable Goods Orders measure the change in the total value of new orders for long-lasting manufactured goods, including transportation items. Durable goods orders are expected to gain 1.9% in January after a 2.2% fall in December.

8:30 AM GDP

Gross Domestic Product represents the total value of the country's production during the period and consists of the purchases of domestically-produced goods and services by individuals, businesses, foreigners and government entities.

On the second reading for the third quarter, no revision is expected, with GDP growth at a 2.3 percent rate versus 2.3 percent in the advance report.

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

The call is 224,000 as forecasters look for claims to rise from 219K in the last week.

10:00 AM Pending Home Sales Index

The National Association of Realtors developed the pending home sales index as a leading indicator of housing activity. Specifically, it is a leading indicator of existing home sales, not new home sales.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration Natural Gas Storage report measures the change in the number of cubic feet of natural gas held in underground storage over the prior week. US utilities dropped 196 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week that ended February 20.

11:00 AM Kansas City Fed Mfg Survey

The survey monitors about 300 manufacturing plants selected according to geographic distribution, industry mix, and size in the Tenth District. Survey results reveal changes in several indicators of manufacturing activity, including production and shipments, and identify changes in prices of raw materials and finished products. The Kansas City Fed's Manufacturing Production index plummeted to -9 in January, from -6 in December.

1:15 PM Fed's Hammack: "Financial Stability"

Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack speaks on "Financial Stability" before the Columbia University/Bank Policy Institute: 2025 Bank Regulation Research Conference.

3:15 PM Fed's Harker Speech

Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Patrick Harker speaks on the economic outlook before the Lyons Economic Forecast, presented by the University of Delaware's Center for Economic Education and Entrepreneurship.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System.

Friday - February 28

8:30 AM International Trade in Goods (Advance)

This monthly report offers advance import and export data on the goods components of the monthly trade report. The consensus for goods trade looks for a deficit of $116.0 billion in January, down from a $122.1 billion in October.

8:30 AM Retail Inventories (Advance)

Retail inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by retailers. The advance report is released late in the month for the following month and is part of the Monthly Advance Economic Indicators report.

8:30 AM Wholesale Inventories (Advance)

Wholesale inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by merchant wholesalers and are tracked to gauge inventory change in quarterly GDP.

08:30 AM Personal Income and Outlays

Each month, the Bureau of Economic Analysis also compiles the personal consumption expenditure price index, also known as the PCE price index. This inflation index measures a basket of goods and services that is updated annually in contrast to the CPI, which measures a fixed basket. The January PCE price index is seen up 0.4 percent on the month and up 2.5 percent from a year ago. Core PCE is seen up 0.3 percent on the month and up 2.6 percent on the year.

9:45 AM Chicago PMI

The Institute For Supply Management - Chicago compiles a survey and a composite diffusion index of business conditions in the Chicago area. Forecasters look for the Chicago report to rebound to 41.0 in February.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. The active rig count acts as a leading indicator of demand for oil products. Crude Oil Rigs in the United States increased to 488 on February from 481 in the previous week.

03:00 PM Farm Prices

The Department of Agriculture releases at the end of the month the index of prices received by farmers for the preceding month. Prices received represent sales from producers to first buyers.

10:15 PM Fed's Goolsbee Speech

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee participates in a moderated question-and-answer session before the Stanford Institute of Economic Policy Research 2025 SIEPR Economic Summit.

data.title: Microsoft clarifies TD Cowen report it is canceling US data center leases (update)
data.description:
Update: Microsoft issued a statement to Seeking Alpha clarifying the TD Cowen note.

"Thanks to the significant investments we have made up to this point, we are well positioned to meet our current and increasing customer demand," a company spokesperson told Seeking Alpha via email. "Last year alone, we added more capacity than any prior year in history. While we may strategically pace or adjust our infrastructure in some areas, we will continue to grow strongly in all regions. This allows us to invest and allocate resources to growth areas for our future. Our plans to spend over $80B on infrastructure this FY remains on track as we continue to grow at a record pace to meet customer demand."

Despite Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stating last month that it would spend $80B on data centers this year, with more than half of that in the U.S., investment firm TD Cowen said the Satya Nadella-led company may be canceling some leases.

Shares erased premarket gains and fell nearly 1% in early trading.

"Our channel checks indicate that [Microsoft] has cancelled leases in the U.S. totaling a 'couple hundred' [megawatts] with at least two private data center operators," analysts led by Michael Elias wrote in a note on Friday. The analysts also noted that Microsoft has "pulled back on the conversion of [statement of qualifications] to leases" and has re-allocated a "considerable portion" of its international spend to the U.S.

"When coupled with our prior channel checks, it points to a potential oversupply position for Microsoft," the analysts added.

It is currently unclear if there is simply a delay in the transition from statement of qualifications into a signed lease, or if it's an outright termination, the analysts added. They also said they have not gotten "the level of color" via their channel checks on why this is occurring. They suggested the pullback could be tied to its deal with OpenAI, which may be working more closely with Japanese conglomerate SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) in the future.

"However, as we believe is indicated by its decision to pause construction on a data center in Wisconsin which our prior channel checks indicated was to support OpenAI, there is capacity that it has likely procured, particularly in areas where capacity is not fungible to cloud, where the company may have excess data center capacity relative to its new forecast," the analysts posited.

They also stressed that this is their interpretation of the current situation, and it may change with added channel checks.

data.title: Chevron reorganizes business structure, makes leadership changes
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) +0.8% in early trading Monday after saying it has made changes to its senior leadership as part of a transition to a new organizational structure, in the company's latest move to simplify its operations.

The company said its Oil, Products and Gas organization will be consolidated into two segments, one focusing on upstream operations and the other focusing on downstream operations, midstream and chemicals; Mark Nelson will continue to lead this organization as its executive VP.

The current president of Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) International Exploration and Production unit, Clay Neff, was named president of the Upstream segment effective July 1.

The company's Downstream, Midstream and Chemicals organization will center around key work processes, including Operations, Commercial, Customer and Enterprise Value Chain Optimization; Andy Walz will continue to lead this organization as president of Downstream, Midstream and Chemicals.

Chevron (CVX) also said Bruce Niemeyer, currently president of exploration and production in the Americas region, will become president of the Shale and Tight business starting July 1, and Ryder Booth, VP of the Mid-Continent unit, will become VP of Technology, Projects and Execution, replacing Balaji Krishnamurthy, who has been named president of the Australia business starting April 1.

data.title: ‘Smaller cap’ growth stocks at a reasonable price that are best position to outperform – BMO
data.description:
GMO Capital Markets recommend investors to focus on smaller caps within growth stocks (NYSEARCA:SPYG), (NYSEARCA:VOOG), (NYSEARCA:IVW) that are growing “at a reasonable price” pace.

Brian G. Belski, chief investment strategist at BMO Capital Markets, said that the great outperformance of the high-profile stocks is likely to subside in the next months.

“The run has pushed valuation levels to extremes, making them more susceptible to a downturn in the coming months, particularly considering that the expectation bar is much higher this year compared to the past two,” he said in a note.

Belski added that he sees as unsustainable the significant outperformance of large-cap growth stocks longer term because, historically, the smaller market-cap quintiles of growth stocks have had higher average returns. The best opportunities for outperformance are in the bottom 60% by market cap for growth stocks (NYSEARCA:SPYG), (NYSEARCA:IVW).

“These stocks have expected next-twelve-month EPS growth rates above 10% and trailing only the largest by a slight margin,” Belski said. “More important, this group has also seen an increase in earnings growth expectations over the past six months compared to slight declines for the larger market-cap quintiles.”

Also, the dividend yield spread for smaller-cap growth stocks has risen to historical highs, while dividend growth expectations are still attractive for this year at 5.6%, vs. to an expected decline of 8.8% for value stocks (NYSEARCA:IVE), (NYSEARCA:SPYV).

Lastly, with the possible threat of tariffs affecting trade and economics, “it is common knowledge that growth stocks are generally more exposed to foreign revenue as the S&P 500 growth index (IVW) derives nearly 46% from these sources, compared to 43% for the S&P 500 with the largest growth stocks deriving even more at 50%,” Belski said.

“However, it is a much different story beyond the largest growth stocks, with the bottom 80% deriving only 22-33% of revenue from foreign sources.”

Below are smaller-cap growth outperform-rated stocks that fit this theme, according to BMO analysts.

  • AvalonBay Communities, Inc. (AVB)
  • AutoZone, Inc. (AZO)
  • Brown & Brown, Inc. (BRO)
  • Cincinnati Financial Corp. (CINF)
  • Corpay, Inc. (CPAY)
  • CSX Corp. (CSX)
  • Dayforce, Inc. (DAY)
  • Domino's Pizza, Inc. (DPZ)
  • Ecolab Inc. (ECL)
  • Essex Property Trust, Inc. (ESS)
  • First Solar, Inc. (FSLR)
  • Hess Corp. (HES)
  • Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST)
  • PPL Corp. (PPL)
  • Public Storage (PSA)
  • PTC Inc. (PTC)
  • Republic Services, Inc. (RSG)
  • UDR, Inc. (UDR)
  • Veralto Corp. (VLTO)
  • Vistra Corp. (VST)
  • Workday, Inc. (WDAY)

data.title: Janus Henderson upped to Buy at UBS as net flows turn positive in 2024
data.description:
UBS analyst Brennan Hawken upgraded Janus Henderson Group (NYSE:JHG) shares to Buy from Neutral in a recent note as "early signs of business improvement have begun to manifest in" key performance indicators, including a positive inflection in net flows last year.

Net flows turned positive in 2024 at $2.4B, a huge improvement from the $31B of net outflows recorded two years earlier. Q4 net inflows reached $3.3B, marking the third straight quarter of positive flows, compared to net inflows of $400M in Q3.

The analyst said he expects inflows to accelerate into 2025 and 2026, "given the scale established with some of their key products as well as the enhanced overseas distribution."

He also pointed to the company's stable management fee rate and larger operating margins, despite broader industry headwinds.

Its "differentiated product offerings and strength in the retail channel" is expected to result in sustained, positive organic growth over the years ahead, Hawken wrote in a Feb. 23 note to clients.

UBS's Buy rating diverges from the SA Quant system rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Hold.

JHG shares gained 1.4% in Monday morning trading.

data.title: Gilead’s HIV prevention drug gets EMA validation for parallel accelerated review
data.description:
• Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) announced validation from the European Medicines Agency for the parallel accelerated review of its marketing application and EU-Medicines for all application for lenacapavir.
  • Lenacapavir is Gilead’s twice-yearly injectable HIV prevention drug. The marketing application seeks authorization from the European Commission, while the EU-M4all application would make the drug available in low- and lower-middle income countries.
  • The drug has also been approved for priority review by the U.S. FDA.
  • The EMA reasoned that lenacapavir was of major interest for public health and therapeutic innovation.
  • Lenacapavir is currently approved in multiple countries to treat adults with multi-drug resistant HIV in combination with other antiretrovirals. It is yet to be approved for HIV prevention.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Home Depot Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
U.S. home improvement chain Home Depot (NYSE:HD) is set to post results for the holiday quarter on Tuesday, before markets open.

Wall Street expects the Atlanta-based company to post EPS of $3.04 on revenue of $39.07 billion during the quarter, implying a rise of 12% year-over-year.

Higher borrowing costs and sticky inflation has pressured Home Depot in recent years, as consumers put off discretionary spending. However, the company topped comparable sales expectations and forecast a smaller drop in annual same-store sales in November, a sign of improved demand from homeowners looking to renovate their properties.

“We now expect continued improvement in comps from 3Q’s -1.3% on the back of increased consumer engagement in seasonal goods (holiday), incremental sales from hurricanes - and wildfires -related demand, stronger appliances sales, higher lumber prices and possibly better demand for bigger ticket projects based on remodel market index data,” noted Wedbush analyst Seth Basham.

Piper Sandler analyst Peter Keith said as big-ticket remodel trends pick up in 2025, the brokerage expects this outperformance to increase.

Seeking Alpha analysts still rated the stock a Sell, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings are cautious and consider it a Hold. Wall Street analysts rated it a Buy.

Home Depot lost over 2% so far this year, compared to the over 2% rise in the broader S&P500 Index.

Earlier in November, CEO Ted Decker warned that consumers are holding back on expensive remodeling projects as they wait for loan rates to improve. Basham also anticipate potential headwinds from tariffs that could pressure consumer spending power.

Over the last one year, HD has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 16 upward revisions and no downward revisions, while revenue estimates have been revised upwards 15 times versus no downward moves.

data.title: Conagra Brands downgraded at Goldman Sachs on supply constraints and challenging recovery
data.description:
Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) shares ticked up higher on Monday, last up 1.32%, even after Goldman Sachs downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy, and updated its 12-month price target of $26.

Goldman downgraded CAG shares on what analysts believe is an elongated path of improvement for Conagra, with less visibility. Moreover, GS analysts see an increased focus on frozen by a key player, which will likely make CAG’s recovery more challenging.

Analysts also noted certain continued headwinds tied to increasing competition, higher promotions, and rising input costs.

GS believes recent supply constraint issues delay the sales improvement story for Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG), which recently reduced its FY25 guidance, with lower organic net sales.

Increasing competition in the frozen segment by peers such as Nestlé, which has been expanding its frozen food capacity in the US, further makes CAG’s recovery more challenging, according to analysts.

GS also highlighted input cost pressure, which it believes could linger into FY26. “We have observed further increases in several of its key commodities over the past couple of months, which could lead to incremental margin pressure in FY26,” analysts said in an investor note on Monday.

The financial firm also lowered its FY25-27 EPS estimates for CAG to $2.35/$2.47/$2.55 from $2.47/$2.63/$2.73, respectively, primarily due to the supply constraint issue in FY25 and incremental margin pressures longer term.

data.title: Charlotte's Web connected CBD candidate for autism advances to phase 2
data.description:
• DeFloria, a collaboration that includes Charlotte's Web (OTCQX:CWBHF) and Ajna BioSciences, will advance AJA001, an oral solution containing cannabidiol (CBD) hemp extract for irritability associated with autism spectrum disorder, into phase 2.
  • The trial will examine the the safety, tolerability, and effectiveness of AJA001 in adolescents and adults with autism. Data will help to design larger Phase 3 trials.
  • The phase 2 trial is slated to start by mid-year and has received U.S. FDA Investigational New Drug clearance. It will be an open-label, 12-week study to investigate AJA001 and enroll 60 patients between 13 and 29 years old.

data.title: Block upgraded to Outperform at BMO after Q4 selloff creates attractive entry point
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets upgraded Block (NYSE:XYZ) to Outperform from Market Perform as analyst Rufus Hone sees the potential for investor sentiment to improve through 2025 as Cash App/Square gross profit growth accelerates.

The stock selloff after Q4 earnings reduces the downside risk, provides a more attractive entry point, and means sentiment/positioning is more balanced, BMO said.

"In particular, expectations for Square/Cash App's gross profit ("GP") growth appear more achievable; higher sales & marketing/R&D spend is now contemplated, while GP/adjusted EBITDA estimates have declined low single digit-percentage since 3Q24," Hone wrote in a note to clients.

After Block (NYSE:XYZ) issued soft Q1 guidance, BMO lowered its EPS estimates to $0.50 from $0.98 for Q1, to $2.54 from $4.55 for 2025, and to $3.56 from $5.72 for 2026.

BMO's Outperform rating on Block (XYZ) contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average SA Analyst rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Buy.

Block (XYZ) stock fell 2.7% in Monday morning trading, during a session when most fintech stocks are dropping. For example, Global X FinTech ETF (FINX) fell 1.5%.
